wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the project overview section focused on the Medicentral healthcare monitoring system. Explain its purpose as a real-time patient vitals monitoring platform for healthcare facilities, architecture overview showing the Django backend with WebSocket support and React frontend, and relationship to medical device ecosystems. Document the implementation approach using HL7/MLLP protocols, multi-clinic tenant isolation, and AI-powered risk prediction. Include both conceptual overviews for healthcare professionals and technical details for developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including terms like 'monitoring', 'devices', 'patients', and 'clinics'. Provide practical examples demonstrating the system's role in clinical workflows. Document the system's mission-critical nature for 24/7 healthcare monitoring and its compliance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend architecture documentation for the Django-based backend system. Describe the Django application structure with ASGI/WSGI configuration for production deployment. Document the multi-tenant architecture using clinic-based isolation and user profile associations. Explain the REST API design with Django REST Framework, including URL routing patterns and view implementations. Detail the database design with Django models covering clinics, patients, devices, and user profiles. Include authentication architecture with session-based login and permission management. Document the application settings configuration, environment variable management, and security considerations. Address scalability patterns, database optimization strategies, and caching mechanisms. Provide examples of API endpoint usage and integration patterns with the frontend and medical device layers.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Introduction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the system introduction section focused on Medicentral's purpose as a real-time patient vitals monitoring platform for healthcare facilities. Explain how the system connects medical devices to a centralized monitoring dashboard, enabling continuous patient surveillance in clinical environments. Document the system's mission-critical nature for 24/7 healthcare monitoring, its role in reducing medical errors through automated alerts, and its impact on clinical decision-making. Include the system's compliance with healthcare regulations and standards. Provide concrete examples of how the platform improves patient outcomes through early warning systems and trend analysis. Use terminology consistent with healthcare IT including 'vitals monitoring', 'clinical workflow', and 'patient safety'. Make content accessible to both healthcare professionals and technical stakeholders.

wiki_catalogs.name: Django Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Django application structure in Medicentral. Explain the project organization with main medicentral project and monitoring app modules. Document the settings configuration including database setup, middleware configuration, authentication backends, and environment variable management. Detail the URL routing system covering main URL patterns, monitoring app URLs, and API endpoint routing. Explain the ASGI configuration for Django Channels including WebSocket routing and channel layers. Document the application configuration in apps.py and how it integrates with Django's app registry. Include practical examples of adding new apps, configuring custom middleware, and extending URL patterns. Address Django's security settings, static file handling, and production deployment considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: React Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the React application structure in Medicentral. Explain the main App component architecture starting with authentication checking, conditional rendering for login/dashboard, and routing logic. Document the Vite configuration including build settings, proxy configuration for API requests, and development server setup. Detail the component hierarchy from App.tsx down to specialized components, explaining the authentication flow and session management. Include practical examples of adding new routes, implementing protected components, and customizing the application shell. Address TypeScript configuration, environment variable handling, and build optimization strategies. Document the integration between React components and the Zustand state management system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Environment Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development environment setup documentation for the Medicentral system. Explain the Docker Compose configuration including service definitions for Django backend, PostgreSQL database, Redis cache, and Nginx reverse proxy. Document the container orchestration patterns, volume mounting strategies for persistent data and code synchronization, and network configuration between containers. Detail the environment variable management for different development scenarios including database credentials, debug mode settings, and external service URLs. Explain the Django development server configuration, auto-reload mechanisms, and debugging setup with PyCharm or VSCode. Document the database initialization process, migration execution, and seed data loading. Include practical examples of running the development stack, accessing services through ports, and troubleshooting common development issues. Address performance optimization for local development, hot reloading configuration, and development-specific security settings.

wiki_catalogs.name: HL7/MLLP Protocol Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the HL7/MLLP protocol implementation in Medicentral. Explain the HL7 standard compliance including MLLP (Minimum Lower Layer Protocol) framing with proper start and end markers (0x0B...0x1C0x0D), message structure validation, and encoding support for UTF-8 and UTF-16. Document the TCP socket server implementation including port binding, connection handling, threading model, and graceful error recovery. Detail the MLLP payload extraction mechanism, message segmentation, and batch processing of multiple HL7 messages per connection. Explain the automatic acknowledgment (ACK) system with MSA segment generation, message control ID handling, and error propagation. Include practical examples of configuring HL7 listener settings, troubleshooting connection issues, and handling edge cases like malformed messages and device timeouts. Address security considerations for medical device networks and compliance with healthcare IT standards.

wiki_catalogs.name: React Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the React application structure and configuration. Detail the main App component architecture with authentication flow, conditional rendering based on session status, and component hierarchy. Document the Vite configuration including build settings, proxy configuration for API requests, and development server setup. Explain the entry point configuration in main.tsx and how React components are mounted to the DOM. Include routing patterns, lazy loading strategies, and code-splitting approaches. Document the development workflow, hot module replacement, and build optimization techniques. Address environment variable handling, asset management, and static file serving. Provide examples of component composition patterns and integration with external libraries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Django Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Django application structure and configuration. Detail the ASGI and WSGI application setup for production deployment, explaining the differences and use cases for each. Document the Django settings configuration including environment variables, database settings, static files configuration, and security settings. Explain the URL routing system with main URL patterns and application-level routing. Cover the Django app configuration and module organization. Include deployment considerations for both development and production environments. Provide examples of configuration customization and environment-specific settings management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Main Dashboard Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the main Dashboard component which serves as the central hub for patient monitoring. Explain the component's architecture including the sticky header with navigation controls, filtering system (severity, department, search), and responsive grid layout for patient cards. Document the real-time data management using Zustand store integration, WebSocket connection handling, and state synchronization patterns. Detail the status indicators including online/offline connection status, AI risk alerts, audio mute controls, and privacy mode functionality. Include practical examples of customizing dashboard layouts, implementing additional filters, adding new status indicators, and optimizing grid responsiveness across different screen sizes. Address accessibility features like keyboard navigation, screen reader support, and focus management.

wiki_catalogs.name: HL7 Device Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for HL7/MLLP device communication system. Detail the TCP socket server implementation that handles medical device connections on port 6006. Document the MLLP protocol handling including frame extraction, acknowledgment mechanisms, and connection lifecycle management. Explain the HL7 message parsing architecture with support for UTF-8 and UTF-16 encodings. Cover device discovery and registration patterns, NAT traversal support, and connection handshake procedures. Include configuration options for timeout settings, diagnostic logging, and connection management. Document error handling strategies for various connection states including RST/FIN scenarios and malformed HL7 messages. Provide examples of device integration workflows and troubleshooting common connection issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Consumer Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the WebSocket consumer implementation using Django Channels. Document the MonitoringConsumer class architecture, including connection handling, authentication verification, and clinic-based group management. Explain the connect() method's authentication flow, clinic scope validation, and channel group subscription process. Detail the disconnect() method's cleanup procedures and resource management. Document the receive() method's message parsing, JSON validation, and asynchronous database operations. Cover the monitoring_message() handler for receiving broadcast events. Include examples of WebSocket message formats, connection lifecycle events, and error handling patterns. Address authentication requirements, session validation, and anonymous user handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: MLLP Protocol Handling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for MLLP (Minimal Lower Layer Protocol) implementation in the HL7 listener. Document the MLLP framing mechanism including start byte (0x0B) and end sequences (0x1C0D or 0x1C0A). Explain the frame extraction algorithm that processes incoming TCP data streams and identifies complete HL7 messages. Cover the acknowledgment system with MSA|AA responses and automatic ACK generation for received messages. Detail timeout handling for connection establishment and data reception. Include examples of MLLP frame construction and parsing, error handling for malformed frames, and performance considerations for high-throughput medical device communication.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the Medicentral system focused on rapid onboarding and initial setup. Explain prerequisites including Python 3.8+, Node.js, Docker, and healthcare IT knowledge. Document development environment setup with step-by-step instructions for local development using Docker Compose, manual setup with virtual environments, and frontend development server configuration. Include production deployment guide covering Kubernetes setup, Nginx configuration, and SSL certificate management. Provide initial configuration steps including environment variables, database initialization, and HL7 device setup. Include practical examples of running the system locally, accessing the dashboard, and connecting test medical devices. Document common setup issues and their solutions, ensuring beginners can successfully deploy the system within hours.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend architecture documentation for the React/Vite-based monitoring dashboard. Describe the component hierarchy with main application structure, routing configuration, and state management using Zustand. Document the real-time data visualization architecture including canvas-based ECG rendering for high-frequency data streams. Explain the WebSocket integration patterns for live data updates and alarm notifications. Detail the styling architecture using Tailwind CSS with dark mode implementation for clinical environments. Include the global state management patterns, component composition strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Document the build configuration with Vite, asset management, and development workflow. Address responsive design considerations, accessibility compliance, and browser compatibility requirements. Provide examples of component usage patterns and integration with the backend API layer.

wiki_catalogs.name: Key Features & Capabilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the key features section focused on Medicentral's core capabilities. Document the real-time patient vitals monitoring system including HR, SpO2, BP, RR, and temperature tracking with automatic alarm thresholds. Explain the multi-clinic tenant isolation system, role-based access control, and administrative device management. Detail the AI-powered risk prediction using NEWS-2 scoring algorithms and clinical decision support features. Document the HL7/MLLP medical device integration, WebSocket-based real-time communication, and comprehensive patient history tracking. Include the administrative features such as user management, device configuration, and system monitoring. Provide practical examples demonstrating how each feature enhances clinical workflows and patient safety. Use technical terminology consistent with the codebase while maintaining accessibility for healthcare stakeholders.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models & Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Medicentral monitoring system. Detail the core entity relationships including Clinic (multi-tenant isolation), UserProfile (user-clinic association), Department, Room, Bed hierarchy for facility organization, and MonitorDevice for medical equipment management. Document the Patient model with vital signs tracking (HR, SpO2, BP, RR, Temperature), alarm level system with color-coded severity, NEWS-2 scoring integration, and AI risk prediction fields. Explain the relationships between entities using foreign keys, constraints, and indexing strategies. Document the data validation rules, choice fields for status and alarm levels, and JSONField usage for dynamic configurations. Include database schema diagrams showing entity relationships, migration history, and data integrity constraints. Address performance considerations including database indexes, query optimization, and data lifecycle management for historical vitals.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard & Monitoring Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the main dashboard and monitoring interface components. Explain the Dashboard component architecture including grid layout, real-time data display, and component composition patterns. Document the PatientMonitor component showing individual patient vitals cards with real-time updates, alarm indicators, and trend visualization. Detail specialized modal components including AddDeviceFromScreenModal for device management, AdmitPatientModal for patient admission workflows, and AiPredictionModal for risk assessment displays. Include practical examples of customizing dashboard layouts, adding new monitoring widgets, implementing custom alert systems, and creating responsive grid layouts. Address component styling with Tailwind CSS classes, animation patterns using Framer Motion, and accessibility considerations for healthcare environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed production deployment documentation for the Medicentral system covering Kubernetes orchestration and traditional server deployment. Explain the Kubernetes deployment manifest including service definitions, pod specifications, resource limits, and security contexts. Document the namespace management strategy, Redis service configuration for caching and pub/sub messaging, and ingress controller setup with SSL termination and certificate management. Detail the Nginx reverse proxy configuration including load balancing, health checks, and static asset serving. Explain the deployment automation script including remote SSH connections, Docker image pulling, service restart procedures, and rollback mechanisms. Document the production environment variables, secret management with Kubernetes secrets, and persistent volume configuration for PostgreSQL data. Include practical examples of deploying to cloud platforms like AWS EKS, GKE, or Azure AKS. Address zero-downtime deployment strategies, blue-green deployment patterns, and rolling update configurations. Cover monitoring setup with Prometheus and Grafana, log aggregation with ELK stack, and alerting configuration for critical system events.

wiki_catalogs.name: TCP Socket Server Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the TCP socket server architecture used for medical device communication. Explain the threaded connection handling model, socket configuration for low latency (TCP_NODELAY, SO_KEEPALIVE), and connection lifecycle management. Document the connection acceptance process, peer IP normalization for IPv4/IPv6 compatibility, and NAT traversal support. Detail the timeout configuration system including receive timeouts, handshake delays, and connection keep-alive settings. Explain the diagnostic logging system for monitoring connection health, empty sessions, and raw TCP data capture. Include practical examples of configuring server settings, monitoring connection status, and troubleshooting network connectivity issues. Address performance optimization techniques, resource management, and scalability considerations for handling multiple concurrent device connections.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Zustand-based state management system. Explain the global state architecture with separate stores for authentication and application data. Document the authentication store implementation including session checking, user authentication state, and clinic scope management. Detail the main application store for managing real-time data, device states, and UI state. Include state persistence strategies, subscription patterns, and state update mechanisms. Explain the integration with React components using hooks and how state changes trigger re-renders. Document performance optimization techniques including selector functions and state normalization. Address state hydration from server-side, error handling in state updates, and debugging state changes. Provide examples of state usage patterns and best practices for component integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models & Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Django models and database schema. Detail the core entity relationships including Clinic, Patient, MonitorDevice, UserProfile, and their associations. Document field definitions, data types, constraints, and validation rules for each model. Explain the multi-tenant architecture implementation through clinic-based isolation and user-profile associations. Cover the database migration system and schema evolution patterns. Include model inheritance patterns, custom managers, and query optimization strategies. Document data access patterns, indexing strategies, and performance considerations. Provide examples of model usage in views and business logic.

wiki_catalogs.name: Patient Monitor Widget
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the PatientMonitor component that displays individual patient vitals and status information. Explain the component's adaptive sizing system (large, medium, small) and how it responds to different grid layouts. Document the real-time vitals display including heart rate, oxygen saturation, blood pressure, respiratory rate, and temperature with color-coded alarm indicators. Detail the alarm level system (none, blue, purple, yellow, red) and how visual indicators change based on severity. Include practical examples of customizing widget appearance, adding new vital signs, implementing custom alarm thresholds, and creating responsive layouts for different screen sizes. Address the component's integration with the WebSocket system for real-time updates and performance optimization techniques for rendering large numbers of patient widgets.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Real-time System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the WebSocket real-time communication system built with Django Channels. Document the consumer implementation for handling WebSocket connections, message routing, and connection lifecycle management. Explain the broadcasting system architecture using Redis for pub/sub messaging and event distribution. Detail the routing configuration for WebSocket endpoints and group management for targeted message delivery. Cover connection scaling strategies, performance optimization techniques, and memory management for high-frequency data streams. Include examples of WebSocket message formats, event types, and client-server communication patterns. Document error handling, reconnection strategies, and debugging techniques for WebSocket connections.

wiki_catalogs.name: Broadcasting System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the event broadcasting system using Redis pub/sub architecture. Document the broadcast mechanism for distributing real-time updates across WebSocket connections. Explain the group-based message routing system and how events are targeted to specific clinic groups. Detail the event payload structure, message serialization, and delivery guarantees. Cover the integration with Django Channels' channel layers and Redis backend configuration. Document performance considerations for high-frequency data streams and memory management strategies. Include examples of broadcast event types, message formats, and subscriber management. Address scaling considerations, connection pooling, and fault tolerance mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: TCP Socket Server Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the TCP socket server that handles medical device connections on port 6006. Detail the server startup process, binding to network interfaces, and listening configuration. Document connection acceptance logic, thread management for concurrent connections, and connection lifecycle handling. Explain socket optimization settings including TCP_NODELAY and SO_KEEPALIVE configurations. Cover error handling for connection resets, broken pipes, and network interruptions. Include connection timeout management, retry mechanisms for failed bindings, and graceful shutdown procedures. Provide examples of server configuration, monitoring connection status, and troubleshooting common socket-level issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Medicentral system focused on high-level design and component interactions. Describe the event-driven microservices architecture with clear separation between Django backend, React frontend, and medical device integration layers. Document component interactions showing data flow from HL7 devices through the listener to WebSocket consumers and frontend dashboards. Explain technical decisions including Django Channels for WebSocket support, Redis for pub/sub messaging, and multi-tenant clinic isolation. Include infrastructure requirements for production deployment, scalability considerations with Kubernetes horizontal pod autoscaling, and deployment topology with load balancers and ingress controllers. Provide system context diagrams showing relationships between medical devices, backend services, and frontend clients. Address cross-cutting concerns like security with HIPAA compliance, monitoring with health checks, and disaster recovery with database backups.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow & Integration Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for data flow and integration patterns throughout the system. Describe the complete data pipeline from HL7 medical device communication through TCP sockets to real-time frontend visualization. Document the HL7 message parsing architecture, device integration patterns, and data transformation processes. Explain the event-driven architecture using Django Channels for WebSocket communication and Redis for pub/sub messaging. Detail the broadcasting system for real-time updates and the consumer implementation for handling WebSocket messages. Include data validation patterns, error handling strategies, and retry mechanisms. Document the integration points with external medical devices, including NAT traversal support and connection lifecycle management. Address performance considerations for high-frequency data streams, memory management, and connection scaling. Provide examples of data flow scenarios and integration patterns for different device types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Target Audience & Use Cases
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the target audience section focused on identifying who uses Medicentral and how they use it. Document the primary users including clinicians, nurses, medical technicians, and hospital administrators. Explain how different roles interact with the system: clinicians monitoring patient vitals, nurses responding to alarms and updating patient records, technicians managing device connections, and administrators overseeing system operations. Detail specific use cases such as ICU monitoring, emergency department workflows, post-operative care, and telemetry unit operations. Include scenarios like device integration challenges, alarm fatigue management, and multi-location clinic coordination. Provide concrete examples of daily workflows and how the system supports clinical decision-making. Address the needs of both technical and non-technical users, explaining how the system accommodates different skill levels and responsibilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Endpoints & Authentication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Medicentral REST endpoints. Document all HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for CRUD operations on clinics, departments, rooms, beds, devices, patients, medications, lab results, and clinical notes. Detail the authentication system including session-based login/logout endpoints, user profile management, and clinic-scoped access control. Explain the permission system with role-based access for different user types (clinicians, administrators). Document the HL7 device management endpoints for device registration, configuration, and status monitoring. Include request/response examples with JSON schemas, error handling patterns, and HTTP status codes. Address API versioning, rate limiting considerations, and security headers. Provide practical examples of client implementation and common integration patterns with healthcare systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Zustand-based state management system. Explain the global state architecture including the main store for patient data, device status, and monitoring state. Document the authStore for authentication state management, session handling, and user context. Detail state update patterns, subscription mechanisms, and data synchronization with WebSocket connections. Include practical examples of creating new stores, implementing async state updates, and managing complex state relationships. Address state persistence strategies, debugging techniques with React DevTools, and performance optimization for large datasets. Document the integration between state management and React components, including selector patterns and subscription best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: CI/CD Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive CI/CD pipeline documentation for the Medicentral system using GitHub Actions. Explain the workflow configuration including trigger conditions for pull requests, pushes to main branch, and manual deployment workflows. Document the automated testing pipeline covering Python unit tests, Django migrations validation, frontend build verification, and security scanning with tools like Bandit and Snyk. Explain the deployment automation scripts including SSH-based deployment to remote servers, rollback procedures, and environment-specific configuration management. Document the quality gates including code coverage thresholds, linting checks, and dependency vulnerability scanning. Include practical examples of configuring secrets management, environment variables, and deployment matrices for multiple environments. Address advanced deployment strategies like canary releases, feature flags, and automated rollback on failure. Cover integration with monitoring systems, notification channels for deployment status, and audit logging for compliance purposes.

wiki_catalogs.name: HL7 Message Parsing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the HL7 message parsing system and screen parsing functionality. Explain the HL7 segment parsing including MSH, PID, PV1, OBX segments, and custom field extraction. Document the vitals data extraction process for vital signs like heart rate, oxygen saturation, blood pressure, respiratory rate, and temperature. Detail the screen parsing system for extracting vital signs from device display screens using pattern recognition and OCR techniques. Explain the data normalization process, unit conversion, and validation rules for parsed values. Include practical examples of handling different HL7 message formats, device-specific variations, and error scenarios. Document the parsing performance optimization, memory management, and extensibility for supporting new device types and data formats.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the main dashboard interface and patient monitoring components. Document the Dashboard component architecture including layout structure, grid system for patient cards, and real-time data display patterns. Explain the PatientMonitor component implementation with vitals visualization, trend analysis, and alarm state management. Detail the modal components including AddDeviceFromScreenModal for device configuration and other administrative interfaces. Document the responsive design patterns, grid layouts, and component composition strategies. Include the real-time data binding patterns, WebSocket integration for live updates, and state synchronization. Explain the visual hierarchy, color coding for alarm states, and accessibility considerations for clinical environments. Address performance optimization for large datasets, virtual scrolling for patient lists, and efficient re-rendering strategies. Provide examples of component usage and integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Endpoints Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all REST endpoints in the monitoring application. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for each endpoint. Detail the CRUD operations for clinics, departments, rooms, beds, and devices. Explain the authentication endpoints including login, logout, and session management. Cover specialized endpoints for patient monitoring, device management, and administrative functions. Include request/response examples with JSON schemas, error handling patterns, and status codes. Document filtering, pagination, and search capabilities. Provide client integration examples and best practices for API consumption.

wiki_catalogs.name: Modal Components System

Explain the common modal architecture including overlay management, focus trapping, keyboard navigation (Escape key), and proper ARIA attributes for accessibility. Document each specific modal component: AddDeviceFromScreenModal for device management workflows, AdmitPatientModal for patient admission processes, AiPredictionModal for displaying AI risk assessments, SettingsModal for user preferences and system configuration, and ColorGuideModal for alarm color interpretation. Cover operational best practices for patch management, dependency updates, and system upgrades with minimal downtime.

wiki_catalogs.name: Device Integration Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the device integration workflow from initial connection through data ingestion. Explain the device discovery and registration process, peer IP resolution with NAT support, and automatic device mapping to bed/patient records. Document the vitals payload application process, data validation, and error handling for malformed or incomplete data. Detail the device state management including online/offline detection, connection status tracking, and heartbeat monitoring. Explain the integration with Django models for persistent storage, patient matching algorithms, and bed assignment logic. Include practical examples of configuring new devices, troubleshooting integration issues, and extending support for additional medical equipment. Address the workflow for device maintenance, reconnection handling, and data reconciliation processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Data Visualization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for real-time data visualization and audio alarm systems. Detail the PatientMonitor component's data visualization patterns including ECG waveform rendering, vital signs charts, and trend line displays. Explain the canvas-based rendering techniques for high-frequency data streams and performance optimization strategies. Document the alarm system implementation including audio alerts, visual indicators, and notification patterns. Detail the WebSocket integration for live data updates, data buffering strategies, and handling connection interruptions. Explain the chart rendering libraries, data transformation patterns, and real-time animation techniques. Include the alarm threshold management, severity level classification, and escalation patterns. Address performance considerations for continuous data streams, memory management for historical data, and responsive chart sizing. Document the integration with the backend real-time events and synchronization patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the authentication and authorization system. Explain the session-based authentication implementation with Django's built-in authentication framework. Document user profile creation, clinic association, and role-based access control. Detail the clinic scope system for multi-tenant isolation and data partitioning. Cover permission management, group-based access control, and custom permission decorators. Include login/logout workflows, session management, and CSRF protection. Document security considerations including password policies, session timeouts, and secure cookie settings. Provide examples of implementing custom authentication views and protecting API endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling and Theming System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the dashboard's styling and theming architecture built with Tailwind CSS and custom CSS. Explain the color palette system including the primary emerald theme, alarm-specific colors (red for critical, yellow for warnings, green for stable), and background gradients for the clinical environment feel. Document the responsive design patterns using Tailwind's grid system, flexbox utilities, and breakpoint-specific layouts. Detail the dark theme implementation with glass-morphism effects using backdrop blur and semi-transparent backgrounds. Include practical examples of customizing the color scheme, creating new themed components, implementing custom animations and transitions, and maintaining consistency across different screen sizes. Address accessibility considerations including contrast ratios, focus indicators, and high-contrast mode support for healthcare environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event-driven Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the event-driven architecture that powers real-time updates and notifications. Document the event broadcasting system using Redis pub/sub for decoupled communication between components. Explain the event types, message formats, and subscription patterns for different client groups. Detail the WebSocket actions implementation for handling client requests and coordinating system responses. Cover event sourcing patterns, message ordering guarantees, and fault tolerance mechanisms. Include examples of event flow scenarios, debugging event processing issues, and optimizing event throughput for large-scale deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Device Discovery and Registration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the device discovery and registration system for medical equipment integration. Explain IP address resolution including IPv4-mapped IPv6 handling and NAT traversal support. Detail the device lookup mechanisms using peer IP addresses, local IP matching, and fallback strategies. Cover device registration workflows, enabling/disabling HL7 communication per device, and bed-to-device associations. Document the integration with the monitoring system including device status tracking and online/offline state management. Include examples of device configuration, troubleshooting connection issues, and managing multiple device types. Address security considerations for device authentication and access control.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend development documentation for the React/Vite-based Medicentral dashboard system. Explain the component hierarchy starting with App.tsx and main Dashboard component, including specialized components for patient monitoring, device management, and administrative functions. Document the state management system using Zustand for global state, WebSocket integration for real-time data updates, and data synchronization patterns with the backend API. Detail the styling architecture using Tailwind CSS and component composition patterns for reusable UI elements. Explain the API integration layer including REST client implementation, WebSocket connection management, and error handling strategies. Document the real-time data visualization components showing patient vitals charts, alarm indicators, and trend analysis. Include practical examples of customizing the dashboard layout, adding new monitoring widgets, and implementing custom alert systems. Address performance optimization techniques, responsive design patterns, and accessibility compliance for healthcare environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack & Dependencies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the technology stack section focused on all technologies and dependencies used in Medicentral. Document the backend stack including Django 5.0+, Django REST Framework, Django Channels, ASGI application, PostgreSQL/SQLite database, and Redis for caching and pub/sub messaging. Detail the frontend stack with React 19, Vite build tool, Zustand state management, Tailwind CSS styling, and Recharts for data visualization. Include external service integrations such as Google Generative AI for risk prediction, Docker containerization, and Kubernetes orchestration. Document development dependencies, testing frameworks, and deployment tools. Explain the rationale behind technology choices, version compatibility requirements, and upgrade paths. Include system requirements, hardware considerations, and performance expectations. Provide guidance on setting up development environments and production deployments. Address licensing considerations and open-source dependency management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Communication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the real-time communication system using Django Channels. Explain the WebSocket consumer implementation in consumers.py including connection handling, message routing, and group management for broadcasting updates. Document the routing configuration in routing.py for WebSocket URL patterns and channel layer integration. Detail the broadcasting system in broadcast.py covering event distribution, message serialization, and connection lifecycle management. Explain the WebSocket actions system in ws_actions.py for handling client-server interactions and command processing. Include practical examples of establishing WebSocket connections, subscribing to patient updates, and implementing custom event handlers. Address scalability considerations with Redis channel layers, connection pooling, automatic reconnection handling, and performance optimization for real-time data streaming. Document error handling, connection monitoring, and debugging techniques for WebSocket implementations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration & Troubleshooting Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive configuration and troubleshooting documentation for medical device integration. Document all environment variables including HL7_LISTEN_PORT, HL7_SEND_ACK, HL7_RECV_TIMEOUT_SEC, HL7_SEND_CONNECT_HANDSHAKE, and HL7_NAT_SINGLE_DEVICE_FALLBACK. Explain diagnostic tools and monitoring capabilities including HL7 listener status, connection diagnostics, and raw data logging. Detail common troubleshooting scenarios such as connection failures, malformed messages, device timeouts, and NAT traversal issues. Include step-by-step troubleshooting procedures, log analysis techniques, and performance optimization recommendations. Document the HL7 audit system, connection health monitoring, and automated diagnostic commands. Provide practical examples of resolving typical integration issues, configuring different device types, and optimizing system performance for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the styling architecture using Tailwind CSS and custom CSS. Document the utility-first approach with Tailwind classes for rapid UI development and consistent design patterns. Explain the dark mode implementation optimized for clinical environments with appropriate contrast ratios and eye strain reduction. Detail the custom CSS architecture including global styles, component-specific styling, and responsive design patterns. Document the color system with clinical-appropriate palettes, alarm color coding, and accessibility compliance. Include the spacing and typography systems, component styling patterns, and CSS-in-JS integration where applicable. Explain the build-time optimizations, purging unused styles, and performance considerations. Address browser compatibility, fallback strategies, and progressive enhancement techniques. Document the styling conventions, naming patterns, and maintenance strategies for large-scale UI development.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Communication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the real-time communication system using Django Channels and WebSockets. Detail the WebSocket consumer implementation for handling real-time connections and message processing. Document the routing configuration for WebSocket URLs and channel groups. Explain the broadcasting system for sending updates to connected clients and managing connection lifecycle. Cover message serialization, event handling, and error management in WebSocket contexts. Include connection scaling considerations, Redis integration for distributed messaging, and performance optimization strategies. Provide examples of client-server communication patterns and real-time data synchronization with the frontend dashboard.

wiki_catalogs.name: Medical Device Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive medical device integration documentation for the HL7/MLLP protocol implementation in Medicentral. Explain the HL7 standard compliance including MLLP (Minimum Lower Layer Protocol) framing, message structure validation, and device communication patterns. Document the TCP socket server implementation for listening on configurable ports, connection handling with NAT traversal support, and automatic device discovery mechanisms. Detail the HL7 message parsing system including segment validation, field extraction, and error handling for malformed messages. Explain the device integration workflow from initial connection through patient matching to vitals data ingestion. Document the screen parsing functionality for extracting vital signs from device display screens, including pattern recognition and data normalization. Include practical examples of configuring different device types, troubleshooting connection issues, and extending support for new medical equipment. Address security considerations for medical device networks, compliance with healthcare IT standards, and integration with existing hospital systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: HL7 Medical Device Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for HL7/MLLP medical device integration system. Explain the TCP socket server implementation for receiving HL7 messages from medical devices. Document the HL7 message parsing and validation logic, including MLLP framing and message structure handling. Detail the device integration patterns for mapping parsed data to patient vitals and device models. Cover the screen parsing functionality for extracting vital signs from device display data. Include configuration options for device connectivity, NAT traversal support, and connection management. Document error handling, message validation, and device discovery mechanisms. Provide examples of integrating new device types and troubleshooting common connectivity issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Risk Prediction System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed AI risk prediction system documentation for the NEWS-2 scoring implementation in Medicentral. Explain the National Early Warning Score 2 (NEWS-2) algorithm including vital sign weightings, score calculation methodology, and risk category determination. Document the machine learning integration patterns including Google Generative AI API usage, prediction model training data, and clinical decision support features. Detail the real-time risk assessment workflow from vitals collection through score calculation to prediction generation. Explain the AI prediction modal interface in the React frontend showing risk scores, trend analysis, and clinical recommendations. Document the simulation system for generating test data, validating prediction accuracy, and testing edge cases. Include practical examples of interpreting risk scores, configuring prediction thresholds, and integrating with clinical workflows. Address the ethical considerations of AI-assisted healthcare decisions, validation requirements for medical AI systems, and integration with electronic health records for comprehensive patient care.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations documentation for the Medicentral system covering development, staging, and production environments. Explain the Docker Compose setup for local development including service orchestration, volume mounting, and environment configuration. Document the Kubernetes deployment strategy including namespace management, Redis service configuration, and ingress controller setup with SSL termination. Detail the CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions covering automated testing, code quality checks, Docker image building, and deployment automation. Explain the production deployment process including Nginx reverse proxy configuration, SSL certificate management, and load balancing strategies. Document operational procedures for monitoring system health, managing database migrations, and handling emergency situations. Include practical examples of scaling the system, performing zero-downtime deployments, and troubleshooting common operational issues. Address security considerations for production environments, backup and disaster recovery procedures, and performance monitoring with health checks and metrics collection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security & Compliance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security and compliance documentation for the Medicentral healthcare monitoring system. Explain HIPAA compliance requirements including protected health information (PHI) protection, access controls, and audit logging. Document the authentication and authorization system including session-based authentication, user roles and permissions, and clinic-scoped data isolation. Detail security measures for data encryption at rest and in transit, secure WebSocket implementation, and CORS configuration for cross-origin resource sharing. Explain the Django security settings including SECRET_KEY management, ALLOWED_HOSTS configuration, and CSRF protection. Document access control mechanisms including clinic membership validation, device access restrictions, and administrative privilege management. Include practical examples of configuring secure deployments, implementing security monitoring, and responding to security incidents. Address compliance documentation requirements, security assessment procedures, and integration with hospital security policies and procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines documentation for contributing to the Medicentral codebase. Explain code organization principles including Django app structure, React component hierarchy, and file naming conventions. Document testing strategies including unit tests for Django models and views, integration tests for API endpoints, and frontend component testing with React Testing Library. Detail code review processes including pull request guidelines, automated quality checks, and approval workflows. Explain debugging techniques for both backend and frontend including Django debug toolbar usage, React developer tools, and WebSocket debugging tools. Document performance optimization practices including database query optimization, frontend rendering performance, and WebSocket connection management. Include practical examples of implementing new features, extending existing functionality, and maintaining code quality standards. Address contribution guidelines for healthcare IT development, documentation requirements, and release procedures for medical software systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ documentation for common issues encountered with the Medicentral system. Document HL7 device connection problems including TCP connectivity issues, MLLP protocol errors, and device authentication failures. Explain WebSocket connection troubleshooting including connection timeouts, message delivery failures, and real-time data synchronization issues. Detail database-related problems including migration errors, connection pool exhaustion, and performance degradation. Document frontend issues including API communication failures, WebSocket reconnection problems, and browser compatibility issues. Include practical debugging techniques using Django management commands, SSH-based remote debugging, and log analysis tools. Provide step-by-step solutions for common deployment issues, configuration problems, and performance bottlenecks. Address frequently asked questions about system requirements, supported medical devices, integration possibilities, and customization options. Include escalation procedures for critical system issues and support resources for healthcare IT professionals.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Medicentral Project Overview

## Project Introduction

Medicentral is a comprehensive clinical monitoring system designed for real-time patient vitals tracking in healthcare environments. The system serves as a mission-critical solution for continuous patient surveillance, integrating with medical devices through HL7/MLLP protocols while providing an intuitive web-based dashboard for healthcare professionals.

### Purpose Statement
The primary goal is to provide real-time monitoring capabilities for critical care units, allowing healthcare staff to track patient vitals, receive alerts for abnormal readings, and maintain comprehensive patient records in a centralized, accessible interface.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Real-time patient vitals monitoring through HL7-compatible medical devices
- Multi-clinic support with tenant isolation
- WebSocket-based live data streaming to web clients
- AI-powered risk prediction and alerting system
- Comprehensive patient history and medication tracking
- Secure, scalable architecture suitable for production environments

### Target Audience
- Critical care physicians and nurses
- Medical device integrators
- Healthcare IT administrators
- Clinical research teams

## Technical Architecture

The Medicentral system follows a modern microservices architecture with clear separation between frontend, backend, and data layers:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[HL7 Medical Devices] --> B[HL7 Listener Service]
    B --> C[HL7 Parser & Processor]
    C --> D[Device Integration Layer]
    D --> E[Patient Data Models]
    E --> F[WebSocket Broadcasting]
    F --> G[React Frontend Dashboard]
    
    H[Django REST API] --> E
    I[Django Channels] --> F
    J[Redis Cache] --> F
    K[SQLite/PostgreSQL] --> E
    
    style A fill:#ff9999
    style G fill:#99ccff
    style E fill:#ffff99
    style F fill:#ffcc99
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer (React/Vite)**
- Real-time dashboard with patient monitoring cards
- WebSocket-based live data updates
- Responsive design optimized for 24/7 monitoring
- Dark mode interface to reduce eye strain

**Backend Layer (Django)**
- RESTful API for administrative operations
- WebSocket server for real-time data streaming
- HL7 MLLP listener for medical device integration
- Authentication and authorization system
- Multi-tenant clinic support

**Data Layer**
- Patient vitals and history tracking
- Device management and monitoring
- User profiles and clinic associations
- Audit trails and diagnostic information

### Design Patterns

The system implements several key architectural patterns:

- **Observer Pattern**: WebSocket connections act as observers for real-time updates
- **Command Pattern**: HL7 message processing through structured command handlers
- **Repository Pattern**: Database access abstraction through Django models
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic device resolution and vitals processing
- **Event-Driven Architecture**: Broadcasting system for real-time notifications

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Entry Point**
- `backend/medicentral/asgi.py` - ASGI application configuration
- `backend/manage.py` - Django management commands
- `backend/medicentral/wsgi.py` - WSGI application for HTTP requests

**Frontend Entry Point**
- `frontend/src/main.tsx` - React application bootstrap
- `frontend/vite.config.ts` - Build configuration and proxy settings

### Core Modules

**HL7 Integration System**
- `backend/monitoring/hl7_listener.py` - TCP socket server for device communication
- `backend/monitoring/hl7_parser.py` - HL7 message parsing and validation
- `backend/monitoring/device_integration.py` - Device-to-patient mapping and vitals processing

**Real-time Communication**
- `backend/monitoring/consumers.py` - WebSocket consumer for live updates
- `backend/monitoring/routing.py` - WebSocket URL routing configuration
- `backend/monitoring/broadcast.py` - Event broadcasting system

**Data Models**
- `backend/monitoring/models.py` - Core domain models (Clinic, Patient, Device, etc.)
- `backend/monitoring/serializers.py` - API serialization for frontend consumption

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**
- `backend/medicentral/settings.py` - Django settings with environment variable support
- `frontend/package.json` - Frontend dependencies and build scripts
- `docker-compose.yml` - Local development and staging configuration

**Security Configuration**
- JWT authentication for API endpoints
- WebSocket authentication middleware
- CORS configuration for cross-origin requests
- SSL/TLS support for production deployments

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies**
- Django 5.0+ for web framework foundation
- Django REST Framework for API development
- Django Channels for WebSocket support
- Channels Redis for distributed messaging
- Google Generative AI for predictive analytics
- Pillow for image processing

**Frontend Dependencies**
- React 19 for component-based UI
- Zustand for state management
- Framer Motion for animations
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- Recharts for data visualization

### Integration Points

**HL7 Device Integration**
- TCP socket communication on port 6006
- MLLP protocol compliance
- Automatic device discovery and registration
- NAT traversal support for remote connections

**WebSocket Communication**
- Real-time patient data streaming
- Group-based broadcasting per clinic
- Connection lifecycle management
- Automatic reconnection handling

**External Services**
- Redis for WebSocket channel persistence
- PostgreSQL for production data storage
- Kubernetes for container orchestration
- Nginx for reverse proxy and load balancing

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Device as Medical Device
    participant HL7 as HL7 Listener
    participant Parser as HL7 Parser
    participant Integrator as Device Integrator
    participant Broadcast as Broadcast System
    participant WebSocket as WebSocket Server
    participant Frontend as React Dashboard
    
    Device->>+HL7: TCP Connection (Port 6006)
    HL7->>+Parser: Raw HL7 Message
    Parser->>+Integrator: Parsed Vitals Data
    Integrator->>+Broadcast: Processed Updates
    Broadcast->>+WebSocket: Real-time Events
    WebSocket->>+Frontend: Live Data Updates
    Frontend->>+WebSocket: User Actions
    WebSocket->>+Broadcast: Action Events
    Broadcast->>+Integrator: Command Execution
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Real-time Patient Monitoring**
- Live vitals tracking (HR, SpO2, BP, RR, Temperature)
- Visual alarm indicators with color-coded severity levels
- Automatic trend analysis and anomaly detection
- AI-powered risk prediction system

**Multi-clinic Management**
- Tenant isolation for healthcare facilities
- Role-based access control
- Department and room-level organization
- Device assignment and management

**Comprehensive Patient Records**
- Complete medical history tracking
- Medication administration logs
- Laboratory results integration
- Clinical notes and documentation

### Implementation Highlights

**HL7 Device Integration**
- `backend/monitoring/hl7_listener.py` - Robust TCP socket server with automatic retry logic
- `backend/monitoring/device_integration.py` - Intelligent device-to-patient matching
- `backend/monitoring/hl7_parser.py` - Flexible HL7 message parsing supporting multiple formats

**Real-time Dashboard**
- `frontend/src/components/Dashboard.tsx` - Responsive grid layout with filtering capabilities
- `frontend/src/components/PatientMonitor.tsx` - Individual patient monitoring cards
- `frontend/src/store.ts` - Centralized state management with WebSocket integration

**AI Risk Prediction**
- `backend/monitoring/simulation.py` - NEWS-2 scoring and risk assessment algorithms
- Integration with Google Generative AI for advanced predictions
- Real-time risk level updates and alerts

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> DeviceDiscovery
    DeviceDiscovery --> DeviceRegistered: HL7 Message Received
    DeviceRegistered --> VitalsProcessing: Valid Vitals Data
    VitalsProcessing --> Normal: Within Limits
    VitalsProcessing --> Alert: Abnormal Reading
    Normal --> VitalsProcessing: Next Reading
    Alert --> VitalsProcessing: Reading Improved
    VitalsProcessing --> DeviceRegistered: Device Disconnected
    DeviceRegistered --> DeviceDiscovery: Device Reconnected
    Alert --> CriticalCare: Severe Abnormality
    CriticalCare --> Alert: Condition Stabilized
```

**Development Workflow**
- GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with automated testing
- Docker-based development and production deployment
- Kubernetes support for scalable cloud deployments
- Comprehensive health checks and monitoring

**Security Features**
- End-to-end encryption for sensitive medical data
- Audit logging for all administrative actions
- Role-based permissions and access controls
- Secure WebSocket authentication

The Medicentral system represents a comprehensive solution for modern healthcare monitoring, combining robust medical device integration with a user-friendly interface designed specifically for 24/7 clinical environments.

Sources:
- [README.md](README.md)
- [architecture.md](architecture.md)
- [settings.py](backend/medicentral/settings.py)
- [asgi.py](backend/medicentral/asgi.py)
- [models.py](backend/monitoring/models.py)
- [routing.py](backend/monitoring/routing.py)
- [consumers.py](backend/monitoring/consumers.py)
- [App.tsx](frontend/src/App.tsx)
- [Dashboard.tsx](frontend/src/components/Dashboard.tsx)
- [docker-compose.yml](docker-compose.yml)
- [deployment.yaml](k8s/deployment.yaml)
- [hl7_listener.py](backend/monitoring/hl7_listener.py)
- [device_integration.py](backend/monitoring/device_integration.py)
- [requirements.txt](backend/requirements.txt)
- [package.json](frontend/package.json)

</blog>
